deployment_plan.name: Pre-deployment
tasks: Confirm the approved KAIROS artifacts for EPIC-00 and EPIC-01 are present and unchanged at .kairos/01-requirements.json through .kairos/05-test-verification.json.
Verify the milestone scope is limited to repo-root governance docs, schema contracts, example JSON, and Unity EditMode tests, with the Unity project rooted under WhatIfSimulator/.
Audit all newly added content under WhatIfSimulator/Assets/ for missing Unity .meta files and generate/commit the required .meta files before any merge.
Check that canonical contract and example paths exist under WhatIfSimulator/Assets/Data and that README.md and CONTRIBUTING.md point to the nested Unity project correctly.
Confirm no unapproved external dependencies were introduced and that WhatIfSimulator/Packages/manifest.json remains unchanged unless separately approved.
Prepare a merge checklist that blocks release readiness if either of the known high-severity review issues remains unresolved.

deployment_plan.name: Merge Gates
tasks: Require a clean diff review confirming the new foundation files are documentation, contracts, example JSON, tests, and Unity folder scaffolding only, with no unintended engine or gameplay implementation.
Require explicit closure of the missing .meta files finding by checking that every newly added Asset folder and file in WhatIfSimulator/Assets has a committed matching .meta file.

Require executed Unity EditMode test evidence from a real Unity environment because the current shell could not run the Unity CLI and .kairos/05-test-verification.json reports coverage_status FAIL and coverage_percentage 0.
Require attachment of test artifacts to the milestone record, at minimum Unity Test Runner results and a brief note stating Unity version, platform, and test mode used.

Require confirmation that example JSON still matches the documented schema contracts and JsonUtility-compatible shapes, including impactEntries arrays and discriminated scenario modification payloads.
Require maintainer confirmation that branch protection for master has been checked in GitHub settings as an out-of-repo action, even if enforcement is completed in parallel to the code merge.

deployment_plan.name: Unity Verification
tasks: Open the nested Unity project at WhatIfSimulator/ in the intended Unity editor version and allow a full asset import to complete.
Confirm Unity does not regenerate unexpected asset metadata, rename folders, or create new GUIDs for the newly added Core, Data, LLM, UI, and Tests trees.
Run the EditMode test suite from the Unity Test Runner for the new schema and governance tests and capture pass/fail output.
If Unity coverage tooling is available, collect coverage for the EditMode suite; if not available, record that limitation explicitly and still require executed test evidence.
Spot-check that the example JSON assets and contract documents remain in the expected folders after import and that no serialization or text asset issues appear in the Unity editor.
Review console output for import errors, missing references, or path issues tied to the nested project structure and resolve them before merge.

deployment_plan.name: Merge And Rollout
tasks: Merge only after the .meta file fix and Unity-executed test evidence are both present and reviewed by a maintainer.
Use the merge to establish README.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE, and WhatIfSimulator/Assets/Data as the canonical foundation for future contributor and contract work.
Tag or annotate the merge as the EPIC-00 and EPIC-01 foundation milestone so later work references this commit as the baseline contract surface.
Update any milestone or release notes to state that this is a repository foundation rollout for Unity/docs/schema readiness, not a production deployment.
Record the GitHub settings check result for master branch protection in the milestone notes so maintainers can verify the governance baseline outside the repository contents.

deployment_plan.name: Post-merge Validation
tasks: Re-open the merged branch or the default branch in Unity and confirm the project still imports cleanly from a fresh checkout.
Verify the canonical docs and examples are the versions visible on the default branch and that contributors can find the contract surface under WhatIfSimulator/Assets/Data without ambiguity.
Check that issue templates are active in GitHub and that branch protection on master reflects the intended maintainer policy.
Create follow-up issues for any remaining gaps, especially coverage tooling, future validator implementation, or any residual contract wording drift discovered during merge validation.

risks.risk: Missing Unity .meta files for newly added Assets content causes regenerated GUIDs, unstable asset references, and inconsistent project state across machines.
risks.detection: Fresh Unity import creates unexpected new .meta files, changes GUIDs, or shows modified asset metadata immediately after checkout.
risks.response: Stop the merge, generate and commit the missing .meta files from Unity, re-run the import check, and only proceed once the asset tree is metadata-complete.

risks.risk: The milestone is merged without real Unity test execution evidence, leaving the new EditMode tests and path assumptions unverified.
risks.detection: No Unity Test Runner result artifact is attached, .kairos/05-test-verification.json still reflects coverage_status FAIL with 0 coverage, or maintainers cannot state which Unity version executed the tests.
risks.response: Block merge readiness until a maintainer or contributor runs the nested WhatIfSimulator/ project in Unity, executes the EditMode suite, and records the results.

risks.risk: Schema contracts, example JSON, and README terminology drift apart and create an unstable canonical foundation for later engine work.
risks.detection: Review finds mismatched field names, example payloads that do not match contract docs, or repo docs that reference incorrect paths outside WhatIfSimulator/Assets.
risks.response: Treat the drift as a release blocker, reconcile docs and examples in the same change set, and rerun the contract spot-check before merge.

risks.risk: GitHub branch protection on master remains unset or inconsistent, weakening the governance baseline of the foundation milestone.
risks.detection: Maintainers cannot confirm the master protection rules in GitHub settings or the repository still permits bypassing the intended review workflow.
risks.response: Record the settings gap as an out-of-repo blocker or immediate follow-up owner task, and do not declare the governance rollout complete until the settings check is done.

risks.risk: Unity import or test behavior differs because the project is nested under WhatIfSimulator/ and contributors open the wrong folder.
risks.detection: Unity opens at the repo root instead of WhatIfSimulator/, tests do not appear, or asset paths resolve incorrectly during verification.
risks.response: Use the nested project path explicitly in release notes and maintainer instructions, then re-run import and EditMode verification against WhatIfSimulator/.

rollback.trigger: Rollback if the merged foundation milestone causes Unity import failures, widespread .meta regeneration, broken schema/example contract integrity, or contributor-facing docs/contracts on the default branch are found to be materially incorrect after merge.
steps: Revert the merge commit that introduced the EPIC-00 and EPIC-01 foundation milestone from the default branch.
Restore the last known good state of README.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE, and WhatIfSimulator/Assets/Data contract/example files.
Open the nested Unity project at WhatIfSimulator/ from a fresh checkout to confirm the rollback branch imports cleanly and no extra metadata churn remains.
Publish a maintainer note describing the rollback reason, with special attention to docs/contracts regressions or missing Unity asset metadata, and create a corrective follow-up branch.

metrics: Unity EditMode test pass status for the nested WhatIfSimulator/ project
Presence of committed .meta files for all newly added files and folders under WhatIfSimulator/Assets
Fresh-checkout Unity import cleanliness with no unexpected regenerated metadata
Schema/example consistency across README.md, contract docs, and example JSON under WhatIfSimulator/Assets/Data
GitHub issue template availability on the default branch
Master branch protection confirmation in GitHub settings

alert_thresholds.unity_editmode_tests: Alert if any EditMode test fails or if no executed Unity test artifact exists for the milestone.
alert_thresholds.asset_meta_completeness: Alert if any newly added Asset file or folder is missing a committed .meta file.
alert_thresholds.unity_import_cleanliness: Alert if a fresh Unity import produces unexpected file changes, regenerated .meta files, or import errors.
alert_thresholds.schema_contract_consistency: Alert if any example JSON no longer matches its documented contract fields or documented canonical path.
alert_thresholds.github_issue_templates: Alert if bug report, feature request, or library approval templates are not visible on the default branch.
alert_thresholds.branch_protection_check: Alert if maintainers cannot confirm master branch protection settings at the time the milestone is declared canonical.
